codeReview.summary: Single commit adds 399-line test file (agent/src/__tests__/cross-area-integration.test.ts) with 11 tests covering canceled-task scheduler exclusion (VAL-CROSS-002), backend status filtering (VAL-CROSS-001), and status lifecycle assertions. The core scheduler-exclusion tests are well-constructed: they verify getDueReminders filters by status='pending' (inherently excluding canceled), sendDueReminders sends nothing when no pending tasks exist, grace-period and EOD-summary checks ignore canceled tasks, the full tick cycle produces zero actions with all-canceled data, and a race-condition scenario (task canceled via web between query and send) is caught by the fresh status re-check in sendDueReminders. The web dashboard was verified via interactive browser testing (all sidebar links, error/empty states, status badge rendering) and build/typecheck passing. No production code was changed, which is appropriate for a verification/integration-glue feature.

issues.description: The test 'web dashboard query pattern (select *) fetches all statuses' creates a local array and filters it — it does not exercise any actual web or Supabase code. The test name implies verification of the dashboard query pattern, but it is purely conceptual. The real verification was done via browser testing and successful web build, so this is not blocking.

The tests 'all five status values are valid todo statuses' and 'canceled tasks remain in database and are visible in history' are trivial assertions on locally constructed objects (makeTodo). They don't exercise any integration point. They add limited value but are not harmful.

The buildMockClient helper uses a complex Proxy pattern while simpler createMockSupabaseClient from helpers.js is also used elsewhere in the same file. Two distinct mocking strategies in one file adds cognitive overhead. Not blocking but could benefit from consolidation.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The feature's skillName is 'frontend-worker' but no such skill exists at .factory/skills/frontend-worker/SKILL.md. The worker confirmed this in skillFeedback.suggestedChanges and proceeded with mission-worker-base only. The feature config should reference an existing skill or the frontend-worker skill should be created.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: features.json has skillName: 'frontend-worker'. Glob for .factory/skills/frontend-worker/SKILL.md returned no results. Worker's skillFeedback.deviations[0] confirms: 'The frontend-worker skill does not exist in the available skills list.'

sharedStateObservations.observation: The interactive browser checks tested error states (Supabase connection failure) rather than true empty states (zero data after successful query). The feature description asks for 'first-visit empty state: with zero data, all pages show coherent empty states', but without a configured Supabase instance, only the error path was exercised. The web code does have separate isEmpty checks (dashboard-client.tsx:222, todo-board.tsx:323, logs/page.tsx:153) that would handle zero-data differently from connection errors, but these paths were not directly verified.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: Handoff interactive checks show: 'Shows Supabase config error message in red box — graceful error handling, no crash or undefined values.' This is the error path, not the empty-data path. The web code has distinct isEmpty && (...) blocks with data-testid='empty-state' that would render different UI.

summary: Pass. The core deliverable — 11 agent tests proving canceled tasks are excluded from all scheduler operations (VAL-CROSS-002) — is well-implemented with meaningful coverage including a race-condition scenario. Web dashboard integration was verified through successful build, typecheck, and interactive browser testing confirming sidebar navigation, status badge rendering, and graceful error handling on all 4 pages. Three non-blocking issues noted: some 'integration' tests are purely conceptual (local array filtering rather than exercising real code), and there are two distinct mocking approaches in one file. Two shared-state observations: the frontend-worker skill referenced in feature config doesn't exist, and empty-state verification covered error states but not true zero-data states.
